stories.headline: Midjourney Unveils Its First Hardware Product: A 60-Second Full-Body Ultrasound Scanner
stories.key_takeaway: Midjourney launched a medical division and a Butterfly-powered full-body ultrasound scanner that aims for 60-second MRI-rivaling scans, but the prototype still runs 20 minutes, uses no AI, and has no FDA clearance.

pull_quote.text: We're not even using any AI in this yet, just really cool hardware and software.

stories.article_excerpt: The company best known for conjuring photorealistic images from text prompts now wants to image something far more personal: the inside of your body.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Cuts Off Hong Kong Staff From Anthropic's Claude, Following Goldman Sachs
stories.key_takeaway: JPMorgan became the second Wall Street bank after Goldman Sachs to block Claude for its Hong Kong staff over Anthropic's licensing terms, even as those same banks prepare to lead Anthropic's IPO.

pull_quote.role: Company terms, on excluding Greater China usage

stories.article_excerpt: JPMorgan pulled Anthropic's Claude from its Hong Kong staff's approved-AI list over licensing terms, following Goldman Sachs.

stories.headline: NeuralTrust Raises $20 Million to Secure the Enterprise's Growing Swarm of AI Agents
stories.key_takeaway: NeuralTrust's $20M seed -- billed as Europe's largest cybersecurity seed -- signals that securing and governing enterprise AI agents is fast becoming its own category: an 'agentic identity' layer akin to IAM for autonomous software.

pull_quote.text: AI agents are entering enterprise infrastructure faster than security teams can adapt, so the window to establish control is now.

stories.article_excerpt: Most large companies cannot tell you how many AI agents are running inside their walls right now -- the blind spot NeuralTrust just raised $20 million to fix.

stories.headline: ByteDance Has Quietly Become Microsoft's Biggest AI Customer, on Track to Spend Over $1 Billion a Year on Azure
stories.key_takeaway: ByteDance, TikTok's parent, has quietly been Microsoft's largest AI customer and is on track to spend over $1B a year on Azure -- largely consuming OpenAI models that OpenAI itself refuses to sell in China.

pull_quote.text: All Azure China operations are conducted in full compliance with U.S. and Chinese laws and are regularly audited by third parties.

stories.article_excerpt: Washington spent two years curbing Chinese access to US AI -- while one of the largest such relationships sat inside Microsoft's own books: ByteDance.

stories.headline: South Korean and Taiwanese Suppliers Reap the AI Hardware Boom as US Curbs Sideline China
stories.key_takeaway: US export controls have turned the same Korean and Taiwanese suppliers that once powered China's smartphone industry into the indispensable -- and dangerously concentrated -- beneficiaries of the AI hardware buildout.

pull_quote.text: There certainly is risk with market concentration.
pull_quote.role: On the dominance of a few Korean and Taiwanese chipmakers

stories.article_excerpt: When South Korea's Kospi punched through 9,000 for the first time, the catalyst wasn't software. It was a memory chip.

stories.headline: Zhipu's Open-Weights GLM-5.2 Tops the Intelligence Index and Undercuts GPT-5.5 on Coding
stories.key_takeaway: Z.ai's MIT-licensed, self-hostable GLM-5.2 tops the open-weights Intelligence Index at 51 and matches or beats GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding at roughly one-sixth the running cost, pressuring closed-model pricing amid US export controls.

pull_quote.text: Introducing GLM-5.2: Frontier Intelligence, Open Weights.

stories.article_excerpt: A Chinese lab just shipped a model anyone can download for free that beats OpenAI's flagship on long-horizon coding tasks at roughly one-sixth the cost.

stories.headline: OpenAI Upgrades GPT-Rosalind, Its Life-Sciences Model Built for Drug Discovery
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's upgraded GPT-Rosalind fuses GPT-5.5's agentic coding with specialized drug-discovery reasoning, runs ~31% more token-efficient on genomics, and ships in trusted-access preview with Novo Nordisk as an early partner amid live biosecurity concerns.

pull_quote.text: To deliver meaningful value for researchers, advanced AI models must be grounded in trusted scientific data, connected to validated tools, and integrated into the real-world workflows researchers use every day.
pull_quote.role: Group VP of AI & Digital Innovation, Novo Nordisk

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI has overhauled GPT-Rosalind, the model it built for drug discovery, fusing GPT-5.5's agentic coding with sharper reasoning in medicinal chemistry, genomics, and the wet lab.

stories.headline: Google's Antigravity CLI Replaces the Gemini CLI as the Legacy Model Catalog Gets Cleared
stories.key_takeaway: Google retired the open-source Gemini CLI on June 18 in favor of the closed-source Antigravity CLI while deprecating Gemini 2.0, image, and video preview models -- forcing developers onto the Gemini 3.5 agentic stack under tight, overlapping deadlines.

pull_quote.text: The new model has been a game-changer for us internally at Google. We've been using 3.5 Flash with the reimagined version of our agent-first development platform, Antigravity.

stories.article_excerpt: On June 18, 2026, the gemini command thousands of developers wired into their terminals and CI/CD pipelines stopped serving requests.

stories.headline: 'Continuous Audio Thinking' Aims to Give Large Audio Language Models a Real Inner Voice
stories.key_takeaway: A new arXiv preprint gives audio language models a continuous latent workspace, grounded by distillation from audio experts, so they can reason over acoustic detail like prosody and emotion before collapsing sound into text.
stories.article_excerpt: Ask a voice assistant to name a song's genre and something lossy happens: by the time it reasons, it's thinking about a text description of sound, not the sound itself.

stories.headline: OpenAI's LifeSciBench Grades AI on Real Life-Science Research -- and the Best Model Passes Just 36%
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's LifeSciBench, a 750-task benchmark built with 173 PhD scientists, shows the best AI model passes only 36.1% -- and drops to 28.1% on tasks with attached data files, exposing how far models remain from real lab reasoning.
stories.article_excerpt: When OpenAI handed its models 750 research problems written by working biotech and pharma scientists, the best system cleared barely a third.

stories.headline: Pew: Nearly Half of US Adults Now Use AI Chatbots, but Trust in AI's Impact Stays Low
stories.key_takeaway: About 49% of US adults now use AI chatbots (up from a third in 2024) and 44% use ChatGPT specifically, yet most distrust AI's societal impact and doubt government can regulate it -- adoption is rising while trust is not.

pull_quote.text: Americans are increasingly using chatbots and bringing AI into their homes, but they have a complex relationship with AI. They may use it, but they're still highly skeptical of it and how it will impact our society.
pull_quote.role: Associate Director of Research, Pew Research Center

stories.article_excerpt: About half of US adults now report using an AI chatbot, and one in four reach for one daily -- yet they remain unconvinced the technology is good for the country.

stories.headline: NAVI-Orbital Puts a Zero-Shot Vision-Language Model in Space for Autonomous Earth Observation
stories.key_takeaway: Researchers ran an edge-optimized vision-language model onboard a satellite, answering natural-language Earth-observation queries in orbit without downlinking raw imagery -- a first that points past the satellite-bandwidth bottleneck.

pull_quote.text: It opens the door to always-on patrol layers in space. If you have a VLM, you can have logic -- like 'monitor this border for me, and let me know when something is suspicious.'

stories.article_excerpt: For the first time, an Earth observation satellite has answered a plain-English question about the world below it -- without sending a single pixel back first.

stories.headline: Gaussian Mixture Attention Offers a Linear-Time Path Around the Transformer's Core Bottleneck
stories.key_takeaway: Gaussian Mixture Attention routes tokens through K learned Gaussian components and a K-slot latent memory, cutting attention's dominant cost from O(N^2) to O(NK); it beats linear and random-feature attention on WikiText-103 but trails optimized SDPA and Mamba.
stories.article_excerpt: Every Transformer carries a hidden tax: comparing all pairs of tokens, a cost that grows with the square of the sequence length. A new preprint proposes a way around it.

stories.headline: A South Korean Telecom's Alleged China Ties Helped Trigger Washington's Crackdown on Anthropic's Mythos
stories.key_takeaway: Suspicion over SK Telecom's alleged China ties prompted the White House to order Anthropic to revoke the carrier's Mythos access; combined with a Fable 5 jailbreak warning, it escalated into the first US export controls on a deployed frontier model.

pull_quote.text: We believe this is a misunderstanding and are working to restore access as soon as possible.
pull_quote.role: Statement after pulling Mythos 5 and Fable 5 offline

stories.article_excerpt: When Anthropic pulled its two most powerful models offline on June 12, it framed it as a regulatory accident. Reporting suggests it began with one customer.

stories.headline: At the G7's Evian Summit, AI Chiefs and World Leaders Strike a Voluntary Deal Centered on Youth Safety
stories.key_takeaway: All three frontier AI CEOs sat at one G7 table and helped produce a voluntary deal centered on protecting minors online, while binding regulation and data-center sustainability fell to US resistance -- crystallizing the US-EU split in AI governance.

pull_quote.text: The situation we're in collectively right now with Mythos and Fable is something that can happen with over-reliance on certain models.

stories.article_excerpt: When the 52nd G7 summit closed on June 17 in Evian, the most consequential photograph wasn't of seven heads of state. It was of a lunch table.
